source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Scenario Modeling
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Maybern is transforming the way private fund managers effectively manage their funds through cutting edge technology: an operating system with deterministic guard rails underneath, agents on top. Maybern is founded by top engineering experts with deep knowledge of the fund management space.

Private funds manage $15T in capital and are growing at 20% YoY, but with increasing regulatory scrutiny and investor demands for transparency, the need for more modern, fully agentic solutions to help private fund CFOs is crucial.

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We are looking for a seasoned product-focused engineer to help build and develop our Scenario Modeling product. The problems we solve are highly complex and require a product driven mindset, expert level technical skills and business acumen to drive the best outcomes for our customers.  In order to be successful in this role, you’ll contribute ideas to the product and collaborate with the team on technical decisions.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

To thrive in this role, you must be an entrepreneurial engineer who enjoys getting into the weeds.

 - Build: Build, test, and deploy software in a continuous manner

 - Lead: Architect, scope, and build core parts of our system from the ground up

 - Partner: Work closely with problem experts to design elegant, flexible solutions that solve our early clients' problems while being forward-thinking about future use cases




THIS COULD BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU...

 - Have 2+ years of experience working with server-side languages (e.g. Python, Ruby, Java) and web frameworks (e.g. Django, Rails)

 - Experience with one of the following Javascript frameworks is desired: Angular, React, Ember, and Backbone. React preferred

 - Solid grasp of relational databases and transactions.

 - Knowledge of platform/cloud technologies and distributed systems. AWS experience is a bonus.

 - Are obsessed with details and excited to "get into the weeds"

 - Have excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Capacity to work in high growth, fast-paced environments, and can adapt to change

Candidates must be authorized to work in the U.S. without employer sponsorship for this role.


BENEFITS

 - We offer a competitive salary + equity package, comprehensive benefits, and a flexible family-friendly work environment. Some of our perks include:
   
   - Comprehensive healthcare benefits
     
     - Maybern covers 100% of medical, dental, and vision premiums
     
     - HSA & FSA plans
   
   - Family-Friendly policies
     
     - Paid parental leave
   
   - Flexible schedule
     
     - Flexible PTO / sick leave
     
     - WFH as needed to fit your needs
   
   - 401k

source_jobs.title: Corporate IT Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Maybern is transforming the way private fund managers effectively manage their funds through cutting edge technology: an operating system with deterministic guard rails underneath, agents on top. Maybern is founded by top engineering experts with deep knowledge of the fund management space.

Private funds manage $15T in capital and are growing at 20% YoY, but with increasing regulatory scrutiny and investor demands for transparency, the need for more modern, fully agentic solutions to help private fund CFOs is crucial.

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Maybern is scaling to over 100 employees and is hiring our first dedicated IT and Security operator. We're looking for someone with a strong sense of ownership and agency who wants to build and shape this function, not just operate within one. The ideal candidate is as comfortable helping a teammate with Google Meet/Zoom connectivity as they are owning the SOC 2 evidence collection process and responding to security questionnaires.

This role is at the intersection of IT operations and security compliance. You will own and operate end-to-end, with a hands-on approach, and be a champion to our engineering team for critical security and privacy matters. As our first full time IT hire, you'll have real input on the tools, processes, and standards we put in place as we scale.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

IT Operations & Employee Support

 - Be the reliable face of IT for every Maybern employee. When something isn't working, you are the first call and will follow through to resolution.

 - Own endpoint management end-to-end: MDM administration (Kandji or equivalent), device provisioning, inventory, and the complete asset inventory lifecycle.

 - Own and maintain our conference room and setup: Google Meet/Zoom Rooms should just work, and when they don't, you fix it fast.

 - Administer identity providers (Okta or equivalent) and enforce access policies across our fleet.

 - Ensure every new hire is set up correctly from their first day across devices, access entitlements, and tools. You will also ensure every departure is accurately offboarded in partnership with People Ops.

 - Set our IT foundations up for success as we scale the business.

Security & Compliance

 - Own continuous SOC 2 evidence collection and control respective monitoring.

 - Conduct and document quarterly access reviews; coordinate audit prep and serve as the primary liaison with external auditors.

 - Administer endpoint security tooling (CrowdStrike or equivalent); monitor for anomalies,escalate and triage incidents with engineering when warranted.

 - Coordinate annual security training completion, track attestations, and flag gaps.

Customer-Facing Security

 - Maintain and continuously improve a security response library to reduce cycle time on questionnaires.

 - Independently respond to vendor security questionnaires (VSQs) and own the security sections of due diligence questionnaires (DDQs).

SaaS & Vendor Management

 - Conduct quarterly access audits across our SaaS stack; manage license governance and monitor for "shadow IT".

 - Own the vendor security review process for software and hardware procurement.

 - Identify opportunities to rationalize the stack and reduce tooling sprawl.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Systems-Minded: You have strong instincts for how internal systems should be set up, and a knack for spotting inefficiencies or risks.

 - Service-Oriented: You are calm under pressure, approachable, and love solving problems for teammates. Y

source_jobs.title: Implementation Manager
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Maybern is transforming the way private fund managers effectively manage their funds through cutting edge technology: an operating system with deterministic guard rails underneath, agents on top. Maybern is founded by top engineering experts with deep knowledge of the fund management space.

Private funds manage $15T in capital and are growing at 20% YoY, but with increasing regulatory scrutiny and investor demands for transparency, the need for more modern, fully agentic solutions to help private fund CFOs is crucial.

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We are looking for experienced fund accountants or implementation experts in the private equity industry that are interested in translating that experience into the development of critical software for the private fund industry. You should have the ability to work effectively with a small and focused team building and implementing complex fund software.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

You will be a part of the foundation of Maybern’s Implementation team, which is designing, building, and implementing our product in conjunction with the Product and Engineering teams. Consequently, you will be one of the chief architects of the product, requiring the ability to translate private equity experience into business logic that we will encode in software. You will also be key in building relationships with existing clients and understanding their unique business requirements.  You will work hand-in-hand with senior members of the team to:

 - Build & Strengthen Relationships: You will be responsible for building and strengthening relationships with our clients, including their CFOs and Controllers.  You will plan onboarding and the roll-out process for new fund managers, and evaluate product enhancements.  

 - Plan: Analyze private equity client needs and key requirements to help prioritize the product roadmap.

 - Design: Work with product managers, engineers, and clients to continue to develop Maybern to solve for client requirements. This will involve implementation of the most complicated fund and investment structures to test functionality and discover product fixes and enhancements.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Collaborative. You love to work in a dynamic and collaborative environment managing both internal and external stakeholders, excel at distilling and incorporating feedback, and are a clear and consistent communicator.

 - Product Vision. You have a deep understanding and experience with the inner workings of fund and investment structures and can translate a variety of structures and calculations in a concise manner. You can apply your knowledge to identify user needs and shape the vision for the user experience.

 - Attention to Detail. There is no room for error when building software to manage such critical calculations for investment managers.

 - Leader. You're eager to shape our client operations foundation and will level up team practices and processes. You can take broad guidance and determine the best path forward. You thrive in a fast-paced environment and are comfortable wearing multiple hats.

 - Strategic. You see both the forest and the trees: think big picture, while also living in the weeds of a problem. In looking at a big picture problem, you pay meticulous attention to each detail while keeping high level implications in mind. You can plan for the long term while navigating short-term hurdles.

 - Curious.  You enjoy learning about different technical concepts and clients’ unique approaches to managing the

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Maybern is transforming the way private fund managers effectively manage their funds through cutting edge technology: an operating system with deterministic guard rails underneath, agents on top. Maybern is founded by top engineering experts with deep knowledge of the fund management space.

Private funds manage $15T in capital and are growing at 20% YoY, but with increasing regulatory scrutiny and investor demands for transparency, the need for more modern, fully agentic solutions to help private fund CFOs is crucial.

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We are looking for a Staff Software Engineer to tackle the company’s most complex, ambiguous, and high-impact technical challenges across products and teams. This role is designed for a strong individual contributor who combines deep technical judgment with strong leadership instincts and thrives in messy, high-stakes problem spaces. You should bring experience taking on undefined problems, driving solutions end-to-end, and raising the technical bar across an organization.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

To thrive in this role, you must be an entrepreneurial engineer who enjoys both solving deep technical challenges and leading teams through ambiguity.

 - Build: Design, architect, and implement solutions to the hardest technical problems facing the company, whether that’s performance bottlenecks, data correctness issues, system scalability, architectural debt, or cross-cutting platform concerns.

 - Lead: Act as a technical leader across teams by setting standards, influencing architectural decisions, mentoring engineers, and improving engineering practices. You’ll step in where problems are most complex or blocked and help teams move forward with clarity and confidence.

 - Partner: Work closely with product, design, and domain experts to translate unclear or evolving requirements into robust technical solutions. You’ll help teams make smart tradeoffs and build systems that are flexible, reliable, and easy to evolve.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Collaborative. You collaborate well across teams, can distill and incorporate feedback, and communicate clearly and concisely

 - Product Vision. You have a full understanding of how to identify the true needs of the user through research, build through a user-centric lens, and create elegant and functional experiences. You have a relentless product mindset that drives you not only to contribute to engineering execution, but to plan, design, implement, and test features to perfect the user experience

 - Leader. You're eager to shape our technical foundation and will level up team practices and processes. You can take broad guidance and determine the best path forward. You thrive in a fast-paced environment and are comfortable wearing multiple hats

 - Strategic. You see both the forest and the trees: think big picture, execute fine details. You can plan for the long term while navigating short-term tradeoffs

 - Deeply Technical. You'll work on systems that process millions of records in near real-time, requiring thoughtful approaches to data pipelines, caching strategies, and distributed processing. Our challenges focus on optimizing complex calculations and ensuring responsive user experiences while handling substantial data volumes.




THIS COULD BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU...

 - Have 7+ years of working experience as an engineer building and shipping world class products with experience handling systems that process millions of records in near real-time

 - Have led product initiatives a

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Fullstack with Frontend Focus
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Maybern is transforming the way private fund managers effectively manage their funds through cutting edge technology: an operating system with deterministic guard rails underneath, agents on top. Maybern is founded by top engineering experts with deep knowledge of the fund management space.

Private funds manage $15T in capital and are growing at 20% YoY, but with increasing regulatory scrutiny and investor demands for transparency, the need for more modern, fully agentic solutions to help private fund CFOs is crucial.

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We are looking for a fullstack, frontend-focused engineer who is eager to dive into the weeds and own solutions for our most challenging projects. The problems we tackle are highly complex, requiring a product-driven mindset, expert technical skills, and a strong eye for polished user experiences. You’ll play a key role in shaping how customers experience Maybern - building intuitive, high-performance UIs that bring clarity to complex workflows.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Architect and build key frontend experiences across the Maybern platform.

 - Work closely with stakeholders across product, design and engineering to design and implement elegant, flexible customer solutions.




THIS COULD BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU...

 - Have 5+ years of experience building and shipping intuitive user interfaces

 - Have deep fluency and expertise in React with TypeScript

 - Have meaningful fullstack experience: from database schema design, through API development, to frontend architecture implementation

 - Have a demonstrated track record of taking ownership in product development

 - Have experience collaborating closely with designers and product managers, with a strong eye for detail and polish

 - Have experience growing, evolving, and maintaining a design system

 - Bonus: Experience working in high velocity, high ambiguity, early-stage startups – especially in Fintech or Enterprise B2B SaaS

 - Bonus: Experience with our frontend technologies: Chakra UI, React Query, React Hook Form, AG Grid, Vite, Storybook




BENEFITS

 - We offer a competitive salary + equity package, comprehensive benefits, and a flexible family-friendly work environment. Some of our perks include:
   
   - Comprehensive healthcare benefits
     
     - Maybern covers 100% of medical, dental, and vision premiums
     
     - HSA & FSA plans
   
   - Family-Friendly policies
     
     - Paid parental leave
   
   - Flexible schedule
     
     - Flexible PTO / sick leave
     
     - WFH as needed to fit your needs
   
   - 401k

source_jobs.title: Principal Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Maybern is transforming the way private fund managers effectively manage their funds through cutting edge technology: an operating system with deterministic guard rails underneath, agents on top. Maybern is founded by top engineering experts with deep knowledge of the fund management space.

Private funds manage $15T in capital and are growing at 20% YoY, but with increasing regulatory scrutiny and investor demands for transparency, the need for more modern, fully agentic solutions to help private fund CFOs is crucial.

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.



What We're Looking For

Maybern is building the operating system for agentic fund management, and Forward Deployed Engineering is where that meets the real world. Our FDE team partners directly with some of the largest private funds on the planet to build sophisticated applications that extend the Maybern platform: production AI, decision support, forecasting, optimization, scenario analysis, formalized workflow logic, and high-assurance automation.

This is not a someday roadmap. We have signed engagements running today and more closing behind them. The Principal FDE will be our most senior client-facing technical leader, sitting at the intersection of technical solutioning, customer leadership, and delivery management. You will sit across the table from CFOs and managing partners who are racing to automate their firms with AI, translate ambiguous, high-stakes problems into structured proposals, lead engagements end-to-end, and set the technical bar for a practice you'll help build and hire. Compensation is designed for outperformance: competitive base, meaningful equity, and a tiered performance bonus where top performers can earn up to 35%.



What You'll Do

 - Lead early-stage discovery with the most senior people in private markets, translating ambiguous and sometimes contradictory needs into structured proposals, and scoping engagements across AI, forecasting, automation, decision support, and platform integration

 - Own senior client relationships end-to-end, from initial discovery through production adoption, managing scope, timeline, and expectations along the way

 - Architect production-grade AI systems that clients run their business on, not demos: agentic workflows, extraction and automation over messy real-world data, evals, optimization, simulation, and decision support, in collaboration with the engineering team

 - Support the sales motion by lending technical credibility to pre-sales conversations and helping shape and close new engagements

 - Build the practice: establish a repeatable solutioning and scoping playbook, mentor engineers across the team, help hire the bench beneath you, and identify when bespoke customer work should become reusable product capability



Who You Are

 - Executive Presence: You are comfortable in the room with senior, non-technical clients. You listen well, ask sharp questions, earn trust quickly, and push back when needed without losing the relationship.

 - Solution Architect & Proposal Writer: You can take a vague client problem, structure it into a credible technical plan, and author a written proposal from scratch: framing the problem, the approach, the deliverables, and the value.

 - Delivery Leader: You have led end-to-end engagements with meaningful complexity, owning scope, timeline, and outcomes across both technical and client-facing work streams.

 - Builder of Real AI: You think past the happy path. You care about how a system behaves when the data is messy, how errors

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Maybern is transforming the way private fund managers effectively manage their funds through cutting edge technology: an operating system with deterministic guard rails underneath, agents on top. Maybern is founded by top engineering experts with deep knowledge of the fund management space.

Private funds manage $15T in capital and are growing at 20% YoY, but with increasing regulatory scrutiny and investor demands for transparency, the need for more modern, fully agentic solutions to help private fund CFOs is crucial.

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.



What We're Looking For

We’re looking for an entrepreneurial and creative Staff Product Designer with 5+ years of experience designing elegant solutions to complex user problems to own design for our Core Workflows and User Engagement squads.



What You'll Do

 - Create design solutions: Embed into the Core Workflows and User Engagement squads, partnering closely with Product and Engineering to break down ambiguous product problems and create design solutions that are delightful for users, feasible to build, and deliver on our business goals. As part of the Engagement squad, you’ll own designing 0-to-1 user-facing features that span our entire application, like data visualizations, task management, notifications, and commenting.

 - Build a UX vision: Work with another designer on the team to define a modern design vision for our product, articulating where our visual language and interaction patterns should evolve over time—and why it matters for users and the business. Partner with Engineering and Product to find ways we can incrementally build towards that vision.

 - Lead discovery & research: Develop a deep understanding of our users' experience in a highly technical domain to identify user needs. Test ideas and refine designs based on direct user feedback and observed user behaviors.

 - Establish design standards: Evolve and expand our design system thoughtfully, introducing new patterns and components that raise the bar while respecting existing constraints and technical realities.



Who You Are

 - Strategic. You build compelling business cases for design investments, connecting aesthetic and UX improvements to outcomes like retention, conversion, and customer satisfaction.

 - Collaborative. You see engineering as a true partner, not a blocker, and you build trust by understanding technical complexity and proposing solutions that respect it.

 - Curious and Creative. You are a deep listener who seeks different points of views, asks the right clarifying questions to understand the problem at hand, and thinks creatively to find the best design solutions. You’re not afraid to go deep to understand user problems in a technical domain.

 - Forward-thinking: You hold a high bar for craft and have a point of view on what "great" looks like—and you're skilled at finding the pragmatic path to get there incrementally.



This Could Be a Great Fit If You...

 - Have 4+ years of design experience, ideally working on a complex B2B Product, and a proven ability of leading design projects from beginning to end.

 - Have a portfolio that reflects your strengths in both user experience and aesthetic design, as well as in the implementation of user testing and research throughout the design process.

 - Are proficient in Figma and experienced in prototyping and interaction design.

 - You’re able to plan for the long term while navigating short-term tradeoffs.

 - Collaborate well across teams; You can distill and incorporate feedback and

source_jobs.title: Implementation Analyst
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Maybern is transforming the way private fund managers effectively manage their funds through cutting edge technology: an operating system with deterministic guard rails underneath, agents on top. Maybern is founded by top engineering experts with deep knowledge of the fund management space.

Private funds manage $15T in capital and are growing at 20% YoY, but with increasing regulatory scrutiny and investor demands for transparency, the need for more modern, fully agentic solutions to help private fund CFOs is crucial.

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.



What We’re Looking For

We're looking for analytically-minded problem solvers who want to bridge technology and fund finance. As an Implementation Analyst at Maybern, you'll implement our software for private fund clients, translating their complex requirements into working solutions while building trusted relationships with fund managers.



What You’ll Do

As part of Maybern's Implementation Team, you'll own the end-to-end implementation process for new clients, working closely with Client Success, Solutions, Product, and Engineering. Your work directly shapes how clients experience our product and influences what we build next.



 - Build: You will build relationships with our clients, plan onboarding and roll-out processes for new fund managers, and evaluate product enhancements.

 - Plan: Analyze key requirements for clients to help Product Managers prioritize the product roadmap.

 - Deliver: You will deliver on key milestones throughout the entire life of the project while managing the relationship with our clients. You will also help to train our clients on our complex fund software, and support them as they adopt our product.



Who You Are

 - Technically Curious: You’re drawn to the intersection of software and business logic, and you like understanding how systems behave under different configurations or inputs.

 - Collaborative. You love to work in a dynamic and collaborative environment managing both internal and external stakeholders, excel at distilling and incorporating feedback, and are a clear and consistent communicator.

 - Detail-Oriented: There is no room for error when building software to manage such critical calculations for enterprise investment managers.

 - Systems Thinker: You approach problems with a technical and holistic mindset, considering broader implications and interconnectivity within workflows and processes.

 - Adaptable: You adjust quickly to changing priorities and conditions, effectively coping with complexity and change, and thrive as a self-starter who takes initiative without needing much direction.



This Could Be a Great Fit If You…

 - Have a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Finance, Engineering, Mathematics, or related quantitative field

 - Have 1-3 years of experience in a technical client-facing role (e.g., technical onboarding, solution consulting, implementation)

 - Are proficient in Microsoft Excel

 - Have strong project management skills

 - Have a heightened attention to detail that can be applied to the translation of a variety of private fund structures and calculations into a flexible software solution

 - Have strong communication skills, both written and verbal, and are comfortable interacting with clients

 - Are comfortable with and excited about using AI tools to enhance productivity and problem-solving in your daily work


BENEFITS

 - We offer a competitive salary + equity package, comprehensive benefits, and a flexible family-friendly work environment. S

source_jobs.title: Senior Client Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Maybern is transforming the way private fund managers effectively manage their funds through cutting edge technology: an operating system with deterministic guard rails underneath, agents on top. Maybern is founded by top engineering experts with deep knowledge of the fund management space.

Private funds manage $15T in capital and are growing at 20% YoY, but with increasing regulatory scrutiny and investor demands for transparency, the need for more modern, fully agentic solutions to help private fund CFOs is crucial.

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

As a Senior Client Success Manager on Maybern's Client Success team, you'll play a pivotal role in owning and elevating the post-sale client experience. From go-live through value realization, renewal, and expansion, you'll take ownership of the client relationship, drive long-term strategic growth, and ensure clients realize the full value of Maybern. This role blends deep financial acumen, executive relationship management, product fluency, and a sophisticated understanding of operational change management.

We're looking for someone with a background in finance who thrives on building relationships, coaching clients through complex reporting and performance use cases, and partnering cross-functionally to drive impact across our business and theirs.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own client outcomes from go-live through value realization, renewal, and expansion; prioritize and manage a high-value strategic book of business.

 - Lead strategic engagements, including regular client reviews, executive check-ins, and QBRs.

 - Drive product adoption and coach clients on reporting and performance workflows, guiding them through Maybern's tools and sharing use case insights from peer firms.

 - Ensure business value realization by guiding clients through change management and aligning on measurable success metrics.

 - Understand and anticipate client needs, including unique fund structures or operational events, and how they map to Maybern's platform.

 - Synthesize product feedback and manage product requests with clear communication and expectation setting.

 - Partner with Sales, Legal, and other internal stakeholders on expansion and upsell strategies and execution.

 - Enable client advocacy, coordinating reference calls, case study participation, and user research engagements.

 - Maintain internal visibility, driving client health scoring, reporting, and leadership reviews that inform proactive client strategies.

 - Mentor and support junior CSMs, contributing to team knowledge-sharing and playbook development.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Client-Centered and Communicative: You are an excellent communicator who keeps stakeholders aligned and advocates for customers through clear, proactive engagement.

 - Domain and Product Expert: You bring deep knowledge of your industry and product space, applying insights to shape strategic decisions and deliver high-impact solutions.

 - Analytical and Solutions Oriented: You solve complex problems using structured thinking, draw meaningful conclusions from data, and make sound decisions in ambiguity.

 - Collaborative and Influential: You build strong, cross-functional relationships and use your influence to align teams around shared goals and advocate effectively for client needs.




THIS COULD BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU HAVE…

 - A solid foundation in private funds; fund finance background a plus but not required.

 - 7+ years of experience in client success, product operations, or s

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Calculations and Allocations

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We're looking for a senior engineer to join our Calculations & Allocations team - the team that builds the core primitives the entire Maybern platform runs on. We own three systems: a custom expression language (MXL) that lets clients define any financial calculation without writing code, a workflow engine that orchestrates multi-step fund operations, and the allocation and waterfall models that determine how every dollar flows through a fund structure. The problems here are genuinely unusual - part language design, part financial modeling, part distributed systems. You should be someone who gets energized by building foundational abstractions that hundreds of downstream features depend on.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

To thrive in this role, you must be an entrepreneurial engineer who enjoys getting into the weeds.

 - Extend a financial expression language: MXL is Maybern's formula system — with its own parser, execution engine, type system, and audit framework. You'll add new functions and calculation contexts, optimize execution, and build tooling that makes complex financial logic composable and debuggable

 - Build workflow orchestration: Fund operations involve multi-step, stateful processes — capital calls, closings, fee postings — that must execute correctly across distributed systems. You'll design the runtime that coordinates these workflows

 - Model fund mechanics: Allocations, waterfalls, carry calculations, equalization — the financial primitives that determine how money moves through a fund. You'll translate intricate fund structures into configurable, auditable models that work across every customer's unique setup
   
   
   
   
   WHO YOU ARE

 - You've built systems where correctness is non-negotiable — financial calculations, billing engines, trading systems, or similar domains where a rounding error is a production incident

 - You're drawn to abstraction design — building configurable primitives that generalize across use cases without collapsing under edge cases

 - You have strong opinions about how domain logic should be modeled and can translate a 30-minute conversation with a fund accountant into a clean data model




THIS COULD BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU...

 - Are comfortable working with server-side languages and frameworks (we're tech agnostic in interviews, but use Python and Django in practice)

 - Have a solid grasp of relational databases and transactions - especially in contexts where correctness and auditability are non-negotiable

 - Have experience leading systems that are highly configurable and designed to handle significant variability across use cases

 - Are knowledgeable about platform/cloud technologies and distributed systems; AWS experience is a bonus

 - Are obsessed with details and genuinely excited to learn the mechanics of private markets (waterfalls, allocations, fund accounting) one workflow at a time

 - Have excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Thr

Private funds manage $15T in capital and are growing at 20% YoY, but with increasing regulatory scrutiny and investor demands for transparency, the need for more modern, fully agentic solutions to help private fund CFOs is crucial.

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.







WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We are looking for a determined Enterprise Account Executive who understands the value of Maybern and can confidently articulate our value proposition to C-level individuals in the private funds space. The ideal candidate can hit the ground running, is capable of working independently, and is excited by the thought of expanding our market share. Top performers excel at generating new opportunities and exceeding quota, while thriving on solving real challenges for clients.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Develop and execute sales strategy in the NYC territory within the private funds market segment 

 - Conduct effective meetings with prospects, in addition to developing qualification sessions to quantify ROI by asking excellent questions and listening

 - Work in close collaboration with leadership and other internal teams 

 - Develop and progress opportunities and close agreements in the banking and investment management markets


WHO YOU ARE

 - Flexible / adaptable: You adjust quickly to changing priorities and conditions and are able to work without much direction

 - Collaborative: You love to work in a dynamic and collaborative environment managing both internal and external stakeholders, excel at distilling and incorporating feedback, and are a clear and consistent communicator

 - Efficient: You’re able to produce significant output with minimal wasted effort; you’re process-oriented and are always looking for ways to improve

 - Persistent: You demonstrate tenacity and willingness to go the distance to get something done

 - Organized: You plans, organize, schedule, and budget time and resources in an efficient, productive manner with a focus on key priorities

 - Leader. You're eager to shape our sales foundation and will level up team practices and processes. You can take broad guidance and determine the best path forward. You thrive in a fast-paced environment and are comfortable wearing multiple hats




THIS COULD BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

 - Have a Bachelor's degree in business administration, finance or accounting or equivalent professional experience

 - Have exposure to and a proficient understanding of the banking or asset management space, or a demonstrated ability to sell into either market

 - Are experienced in selling SaaS products; fintech or banking experience are highly desirable

 - Have 5+ years' experience running the full sales cycle (cold call to close) of a complex solution, using a consultative approach

 - Possess demonstrated knowledge/experience regarding back office banking operations

 - Are experienced with native SaaS solutions, and their unique value relative to on-premise solutions

 - Have a proven record of exceeding revenue targets, including achieving annual multi-million-dollar sales goals

 - Possess superb interpersonal, writing, and presentation skills

*The compensation range for this role is $150k-$170k base salary + $150k-$170k commission.


BENEFITS

 - We offer

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Maybern is transforming the way private fund managers effectively manage their funds through cutting edge technology: an operating system with deterministic guard rails underneath, agents on top. Maybern is founded by top engineering experts with deep knowledge of the fund management space.

Private funds manage $15T in capital and are growing at 20% YoY, but with increasing regulatory scrutiny and investor demands for transparency, the need for more modern, fully agentic solutions to help private fund CFOs is crucial.

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.



What We're Looking For

Maybern is building the operating system for agentic fund management, and Forward Deployed Engineering is where that meets the real world. Our FDE team partners directly with some of the largest private funds on the planet to build high-value systems that extend the Maybern platform: production AI, decision support, forecasting, optimization, scenario analysis, formalized workflow logic, and high-assurance automation. This is not a lab. Engagements are signed and running today, and the systems you build will go into production at firms managing tens of billions of dollars.



We are looking for engineers who can own real customer problems and grow fast doing it. You should be comfortable operating where customer ambiguity, quantitative modeling, AI reliability, workflow design, and product abstraction all meet. The career ladder is defined (FDE to Senior FDE to Principal), promotion follows performance rather than tenure, and compensation is built for outperformance: competitive base, equity, and a tiered performance bonus where top performers can earn up to 35%.



What You'll Do

 - Own forward-deployed engagements from discovery through production adoption, with your scope growing as fast as your independence earns it

 - Architect and build custom systems across AI, forecasting, optimization, simulation, and decision-support workflows

 - Design application patterns that connect Maybern's platform to customer-specific logic, data, and operating processes

 - Build the evaluation, control, and validation frameworks that make AI trustworthy when a client's business depends on the output

 - Work directly with senior stakeholders at sophisticated private markets firms as a trusted technical advisor

 - Turn messy, real-world data into production systems: extraction, agentic workflows, automation, and the QA and nuance that separate working systems from demos

 - Identify when bespoke work should become reusable product capability or shared platform infrastructure

 - Help define standards, technical direction, and operating practices for a function you're joining at the ground floor

Who You Are

 - Architect. You design application patterns that connect platform, customer logic, and data, picking the right level of rigor for the stakes.

 - Customer Partner. You serve as a trusted advisor to senior customer stakeholders and translate rigor without losing precision.

 - Production-AI Builder. You think past the happy path. You care how a system behaves when the data is messy, how errors get caught, and how AI holds up when someone else's business runs on it.

 - Driven by Outcomes. You are competitive, high-ownership, and motivated by impact and upside. You think in terms of the ROI of your work, not just the deliverable.

 - High-Standards Builder. You hold a high bar for clarity and reliability in domains where approximation isn't enough.



This Could Be a Great Fit If You Have…

 - 3+ years of experience

source_jobs.title: Solutions Architect
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Maybern is transforming the way private fund managers effectively manage their funds through cutting edge technology: an operating system with deterministic guard rails underneath, agents on top. Maybern is founded by top engineering experts with deep knowledge of the fund management space.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.





WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We are looking for a Solutions Architect to bridge the gap between the business needs of potential clients in the private markets industry, and the technical capabilities of Maybern’s software. This role sits at the intersection of fund/finance expertise and implementation work -- you'll use your knowledge of private markets operations to translate client requirements into configured, working solutions. You'll be part of a collaborative Solutions Architecture team, working closely with senior Solutions Architects and cross-functional partners as you grow into increasingly complex client scenarios.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Support Client Implementations: Own onboarding and configuration work for a book of clients, with a focus on straightforward implementations and well-defined use cases. Partner with senior Solutions Architects on more complex or ambiguous cases.

 - Translate Business Requirements: Conduct discovery with clients to understand their fund structures, workflows, and operational needs. Map those requirements to how Maybern's platform can be configured to meet them.

 - Escalate and Collaborate: Bring harder edge cases to the team. Participate in collaborative problem-solving with teammates across Solutions Architecture, Product, and Engineering.

 - Contribute to Enablement: Help develop internal resources on technical topics, including walkthroughs, models, and presentations that support the broader team.

 - Partner with Product: Surface patterns from client work to inform product feedback and feature gap documentation.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Fund/Finance Fluent: You have a solid foundation in fund accounting or private equity operations and can engage credibly with clients on their workflows and data models.

 - Client-Facing and Clear: You communicate complex concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences, and you're comfortable running client calls with guidance.

 - Structured Problem Solver: You approach ambiguous situations methodically -- mapping out processes, identifying gaps, and working through solutions systematically.

 - Collaborative by Default: You thrive in a team-based environment and are comfortable asking questions, sharing context, and building on others' work.

 - Growth-Oriented: You're eager to take on more complexity over time and actively seek feedback to improve.




THIS COULD BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

 - Have 6+ years of experience in fund accounting, fund administration, or implementation at a private markets software company or fund services firm.

 - Have an undergraduate degree in accounting or finance; CPA is a plus.

 - Are comfortable in Microsoft Excel and have exposure to fund accounting systems (Yardi, Investran, or similar).

 - Have experience with complex investment structures and financial statements.

 - Are curious about how AI tools can accelerate analysis and workflow -- and are already experimenting with them.
   


BENEFITS

 - We offe

source_jobs.title: Controller
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Maybern is transforming the way private fund managers effectively manage their funds through cutting edge technology: an operating system with deterministic guard rails underneath, agents on top. Maybern is founded by top engineering experts with deep knowledge of the fund management space.

Private funds manage $15T in capital and are growing at 20% YoY, but with increasing regulatory scrutiny and investor demands for transparency, the need for more modern, fully agentic solutions to help private fund CFOs is crucial.

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We are looking for a hands-on Controller who is ready to build. Maybern is a Series B fund accounting SaaS platform scaling from 75 to 120+ people, and this is our first internal accounting hire. You will own everything from the general ledger to investor-ready financial statements, take us through our first institutional audit, and build the accounting infrastructure from the ground up. The ideal candidate is an individual contributor who has done their Big 4 time, has lived inside a high-growth startup, and is ready to own something from scratch. The person who succeeds here will grow into full controllership as Maybern scales toward Series C and beyond.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

You will be Maybern’s first internal accounting hire, reporting directly to the Chief of Staff and Director of Strategy & Operations. You will meaningfully shape our finance function by:

 - Own the Close: Drive month-end close in partnership with our outside accounting firm. Maintain the GL, chart of accounts, reconciliations, and all accounting policies.

 - Build Audit Readiness: Lead Maybern’s first institutional audit for our next fiscal year: auditor selection, pre-audit readiness, PBC preparation, and fieldwork management. Build internal controls appropriate for a high-growth Series B.

 - Drive Revenue Integrity: Ensure accurate revenue recognition under ASC 606 across Maybern’s enterprise SaaS contract structures. Partner with our Strategic Finance Lead to reconcile CARR, ARR, and deferred revenue so our numbers are auditable and investor-ready.

 - Own Technical Accounting & Accounting Policies: Own Maybern’s formal accounting policy positions. ASC 718 (stock-based compensation): maintain the grant schedule, recognize expenses, estimate forfeitures, and handle adjustments for terminated employees. ASC 350-40 (capitalized software development costs): establish the policy and own ongoing capitalization decisions. ASC 340-40 (deferred commissions). COGS vs. opex allocation methodology.

 - Modernize the Stack: Optimize our current tools (QuickBooks, Carta, Rippling, Ramp, Runway) and recommend the right GL and accounting infrastructure for the next 18–24 months. Implement scalable processes and leverage AI tools to operate at a higher leverage ratio than headcount alone would suggest.

 - Partner Across the Business: Prepare monthly financial statements and contribute to board materials. Support fundraising activities and investor diligence. Coordinate cap table, 409a, and equity accounting (ASC 718) with Carta. Collaborate with our Strategic Finance Lead on financial forecasting, department budgets, and strategic projects.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Adaptability: You adjust quickly to changing priorities and conditions, effectively coping with complexity and change, and thrive as a self-starter who takes initiative without needing much direction.

 - Owner. No job is too small. You treat the GL with the same seriousness as the board pac

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Maybern is transforming the way private fund managers effectively manage their funds through cutting edge technology: an operating system with deterministic guard rails underneath, agents on top. Maybern is founded by top engineering experts with deep knowledge of the fund management space.

Private funds manage $15T in capital and are growing at 20% YoY, but with increasing regulatory scrutiny and investor demands for transparency, the need for more modern, fully agentic solutions to help private fund CFOs is crucial.

This is where Maybern comes in.

We are equipping fund managers with a fully agentic operating system for private fund management, enabling private investment managers to focus on what they do best: driving returns for their investors. We are backed by leading venture capital firms and a large number of strategic investors.

Maybern is an NYC based company with a strong in-office culture to drive collaboration, and we are looking to make strong additions to our team.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We are looking for a seasoned product-focused engineer to help build and develop our core product and workflows. The problems we solve are highly complex and require a product driven mindset, expert level technical skills and business acumen to drive the best outcomes for our customers.  In order to be successful in this role, you’ll contribute ideas to the product and collaborate with the team on technical decisions.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

To thrive in this role, you must be an entrepreneurial engineer who enjoys getting into the weeds.

 - Build: Build, test, and deploy software in a continuous manner

 - Lead: Architect, scope, and build core parts of our system from the ground up

 - Partner: Work closely with problem experts to design elegant, flexible solutions that solve our early clients' problems while being forward-thinking about future use cases




THIS COULD BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU...

 - Have 2+ years of experience working with server-side languages (e.g. Python, Ruby, Java) and web frameworks (e.g. Django, Rails)

 - Experience with one of the following Javascript frameworks is desired: Angular, React, Ember, and Backbone. React preferred

 - Solid grasp of relational databases and transactions.

 - Knowledge of platform/cloud technologies and distributed systems. AWS experience is a bonus.

 - Are obsessed with details and excited to "get into the weeds"

 - Have excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Capacity to work in high growth, fast-paced environments, and can adapt to change

Candidates must be authorized to work in the U.S. without employer sponsorship for this role.


BENEFITS

 - We offer a competitive salary + equity package, comprehensive benefits, and a flexible family-friendly work environment. Some of our perks include:
   
   - Comprehensive healthcare benefits
     
     - Maybern covers 100% of medical, dental, and vision premiums
     
     - HSA & FSA plans
   
   - Family-Friendly policies
     
     - Paid parental leave
   
   - Flexible schedule
     
     - Flexible PTO / sick leave
     
     - WFH as needed to fit your needs
   
   - 401k
